channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇷🇴 Russian Drone Strike Hits Romanian Apartment Building
item.description: A Russian Geran-2 drone crossed into Romanian airspace and struck an apartment building in Galati, damaging the roof and sparking fires. Residents describe growing fear as the Ukraine war spills across borders.

item.title: 🇦🇲🇷🇺 Russia Recalls Ambassador to Armenia Over EU Rapprochement
item.description: Moscow recalled its envoy to Yerevan for consultations in protest of Armenia's deepening ties with the European Union ahead of a June 7 election, signaling growing tensions in Russia's traditional sphere of influence.

item.title: 🇲🇲🇮🇳 Myanmar Junta Chief Makes First Foreign Visit to India
item.description: Min Aung Hlaing, who recently transitioned from junta chief to president, is visiting India in his first overseas trip since taking the civilian role, seeking regional legitimacy while continuing a brutal civil war.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Added to UN Sexual Violence Blacklist for First Time
item.description: The United Nations has added Israel to its blacklist of parties responsible for sexual violence in conflict zones, marking an unprecedented diplomatic blow for the country amid ongoing international scrutiny of its military operations.

item.title: 🇨🇩 Ebola Cases Nearly Double in DR Congo as WHO Chief Visits Outbreak Epicenter
item.description: The WHO Director-General arrived in Bunia as confirmed Ebola cases doubled within days, with the outbreak spreading amid insecurity and distrust. Aid cuts are hampering response efforts in the conflict-affected region.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Soldiers Reach Nabatieh, One of Southern Lebanons Biggest Cities
item.description: Israeli ground forces have advanced into Nabatieh, one of the largest cities in southern Lebanon, marking a significant escalation in the Israel-Hezbollah conflict as military talks between the sides begin.

item.title: 🇲🇲🇮🇳 Myanmars Min Aung Hlaing Makes First Foreign Visit as Leader to India
item.description: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing embarked on his first foreign tour since seizing power, visiting India in a significant diplomatic move as the military government seeks international legitimacy.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Accuses Trump of Betraying Diplomacy as Hormuz Blockade Continues
item.description: An adviser to Iran Supreme Leader warned that Trump is betraying diplomacy for the third time by maintaining the naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, demanding authorization for all commercial vessels transiting the strategic waterway.

item.title: 🇦🇲🇷🇺 Russia Recalls Ambassador to Armenia Over EU Pivot
item.description: Russia recalled its ambassador from Armenia a day after Putin warned Yerevan against seeking EU membership, referencing the Ukrainian scenario. Armenia has been pivoting away from Russia toward the West amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇺🇸🇦🇺 Britain Signs Underwater Drone Deal with US and Australia
item.description: The UK, Australia and US signed an agreement to develop cutting-edge underwater military drone technology under AUKUS, expanding defense cooperation amid growing concerns about Chinese naval activity in the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: 🇮🇳🇻🇳 India Signs BrahMos Missile Deal with Vietnam, Nears Pact with Indonesia
item.description: India signed a defense agreement with Vietnam for BrahMos missiles and is close to finalizing a similar deal with Indonesia, as New Delhi deepens military partnerships with ASEAN nations amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇳🇬 Nigeria Puts 21 States on Ebola Alert as Regional Outbreak Spreads
item.description: Nigerian health authorities placed 21 states on alert for Ebola virus importation as neighboring DRC battles a growing outbreak. The WHO confirmed cases have nearly doubled in days, with regional spread concerns mounting.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US Defense Secretary Hegseth Tones Down China Rhetoric at Singapore Forum
item.description: At the Shangri-La defense forum, Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth assured Pacific allies of US commitment to the region while softening previous warnings about China, following Trump's recent Beijing summit with Xi Jinping.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Pentagon Chief Warns US Ready to Restart Iran Strikes If No Deal Reached
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated the US is prepared to resume military strikes on Iran if negotiations fail, as Washington and Tehran work to bridge major differences blocking a ceasefire agreement.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippines Under Severe Threat from China Despite Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: Philippines Defense Minister warned that the country remains under severe threat from China despite recent US-China tensions thawing after the Trump-Xi summit, highlighting ongoing regional tensions in the South China Sea.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Planning Faster Troop Withdrawal from Europe
item.description: The Trump administration is accelerating plans to withdraw US troops from Europe, according to newspaper reports, marking a significant shift in US military posture on the continent.

item.title: 💰 IMF and World Bank Warn Middle East War Straining Global Energy Supplies
item.description: Heads of the International Energy Agency, IMF, World Bank and WTO warned that the Middle East conflict is straining global energy supplies and hitting vulnerable economies hardest, urging coordinated response.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US General Holds Rare Meeting with Cuban Military Officials Near Guantanamo
item.description: A US general held an unusual meeting with Cuban military officials near Guantanamo Bay amid ongoing discussions about regional security and cooperation, a rare diplomatic exchange between the longtime adversaries.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese Scientists Create Kill-Them-All Algorithm for Drone Warfare
item.description: Chinese researchers have developed a new algorithm for autonomous drone swarms capable of targeting multiple threats simultaneously, raising concerns about the future of AI-powered military systems.

item.title: 🌊 17 Nations Launch Pact to Protect Undersea Cables Amid US-China Tensions
item.description: Seventeen nations launched a new international pact to protect vital undersea telecommunications cables, with notable absence of US and China as competition over critical infrastructure intensifies.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Hegseth Softens Tone Toward China After Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth toned down his criticism of China during remarks to Pacific leaders, reflecting a shift in US-China relations following the recent Trump-Xi summit and talks over Iran.

item.title: 🇧🇷 US Labels Brazilian Gangs as Terrorist Organizations, Rubio Announces
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced that Brazilian criminal gangs have been designated as terrorist organizations, marking a significant expansion of US counterterrorism policy to include Latin American organized crime.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Khamenei Adviser Accuses Trump of Betraying Diplomacy for Third Time
item.description: Senior Iranian adviser Mojtaba Khamenei criticized Trump for betraying diplomacy for the third time, continuing naval blockade and making excessive demands in ceasefire negotiations amid ongoing tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Drones Strike More Russian Oil Depots as Campaign Intensifies
item.description: Ukrainian drone strikes continue to hit Russian oil infrastructure, with fires reported at storage facilities in Rostov territory after intercepted drones caused damage, showing ongoing pressure on Russian energy supplies.

item.title: 🇻🇳 Vietnam Leader Emphasizes China Ties While Acknowledging US Importance
item.description: Vietnam's top leader stated that strong relations with China benefit regional peace and security, while also emphasizing the importance of US ties amid balancing act between major powers.
